heading: Operation of battery recharging stations in parking areas used by Federal employees
label: 6364 Operation of battery recharging stations in parking areas used by Federal employees
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Fixing America’s Surface Transportation Act, also known as the FAST Act, and not as part of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act which comprises this chapter.

Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under , Government Organization and Employees.Oct. 1, 2015section 1003 of Pub. L. 114–94section 5313 of Title 5
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 2015 Amendment note under , Government Organization and Employees.Oct. 1, 2015section 1003 of Pub. L. 114–94section 5313 of Title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator of General Services may install, construct, operate, and maintain on a reimbursable basis a battery recharging station (or allow, on a reimbursable basis, the use of a 120-volt electrical receptacle for battery recharging) in a parking area that is in the custody, control, or administrative jurisdiction of the General Services Administration for the use of only privately owned vehicles of employees of the General Services Administration, tenant Federal agencies, and others who are authorized to park in such area to the extent such use by only privately owned vehicles does not interfere with or impede access to the equipment by Federal fleet vehicles.

children.heading: Areas under other Federal agencies
children.label: B Areas under other Federal agencies
children.text: The Administrator of General Services (on the request of a Federal agency) or the head of a Federal agency may install, construct, operate, and maintain on a reimbursable basis a battery recharging station (or allow, on a reimbursable basis, the use of a 120-volt electrical receptacle for battery recharging) in a parking area that is in the custody, control, or administrative jurisdiction of the requesting Federal agency, to the extent such use by only privately owned vehicles does not interfere with or impede access to the equipment by Federal fleet vehicles.

children.heading: Use of vendors
children.label: C Use of vendors
children.text: The Administrator of General Services, with respect to subparagraph (A) or (B), or the head of a Federal agency, with respect to subparagraph (B), may carry out such subparagraph through a contract with a vendor, under such terms and conditions (including terms relating to the allocation between the Federal agency and the vendor of the costs of carrying out the contract) as the Administrator or the head of the Federal agency, as the case may be, and the vendor may agree to.

children.heading: Imposition of fees to cover costs
children.label: 2 Imposition of fees to cover costs

children.heading: Fees
children.label: A Fees
children.text: The Administrator of General Services or the head of the Federal agency under paragraph (1)(B) shall charge fees to the individuals who use the battery recharging station in such amount as is necessary to ensure that the respective agency recovers all of the costs such agency incurs in installing, constructing, operating, and maintaining the station.

children.heading: Deposit and availability of fees
children.label: B Deposit and availability of fees
i
children.text: deposited monthly in the Treasury to the credit of the respective agency’s appropriations account for the operations of the building where the battery recharging station is located; and

children.label: I
children.text: the fiscal year collected; and

children.label: II
children.text: the fiscal year following the fiscal year collected.

children.heading: No effect on existing programs for House and Senate
children.label: 3 No effect on existing programs for House and Senate
A
children.text: under , relating to employees of the House of Representatives and individuals authorized to park in any parking area under the jurisdiction of the House of Representatives on the Capitol Grounds; orsection 2171 of title 2

children.label: B
children.text: under , relating to employees of the Senate and individuals authorized to park in any parking area under the jurisdiction of the Senate on the Capitol Grounds.section 2170 of title 2

children.label: A
children.text: repeals or limits any existing authorities of a Federal agency to install, construct, operate, or maintain battery recharging stations; or

children.label: i
children.text: that has been installed or constructed prior to ;December 4, 2015

children.label: ii
children.text: that is installed or constructed for Federal fleet vehicles, but that receives incidental use to recharge privately owned vehicles; or

children.label: iii
children.text: that is otherwise installed or constructed pursuant to appropriations for that purpose.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of battery recharging stations installed by the Administrator on the Administrator’s own initiative under this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: requests from other Federal agencies to install battery recharging stations; and

children.label: C
children.text: the status and disposition of requests from other Federal agencies.

children.text: the United States Postal Service;

children.text: the Executive Office of the President;

children.label: C

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 7 Effective date
children.text: This subsection shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2016 and each succeeding fiscal year.
